statement: For a basis, surjectivity of `f` is equivalent to the images spanning the space.
lean_statement:
theorem surjective_iff_span_image_of_isBasis
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    {n : Nat} (v : Fin n → V)
    (hv : LinearIndependent K v ∧ Submodule.span K (Set.range v) = ⊤)
    (f : V →ₗ[K] V) :
    Function.Surjective f ↔ Submodule.span K (Set.range (fun i => f (v i))) = ⊤
proof:
Proof: let `v i` be a basis. The range of `f` is a subspace of `V` and is spanned by the images of
any spanning set: since every `x` is a linear combination of the `v i`, every `f x` is a linear
combination of the `f (v i)`. Thus `range f = span{f(v i)}`.
Therefore `f` is surjective (i.e. `range f = ⊤`) iff `span{f(v i)} = ⊤`.
